coverPage.title: Sophie PA Packet Lint Report
coverPage.disclaimer: Sophie Prior-Auth Packet Linter is a deterministic checklist. It is not medical advice. It is not legal advice. It is not coding advice. It does not transmit anything to any payer. Your packet stays in this browser tab; we never see your data. Findings reflect what Sophie's ruleset checks; they are not a guarantee of payer approval. A passing report does not mean the packet will be approved; a failing report does not mean the packet will be denied. The clinical adequacy of every document is the reviewer's responsibility. Sophie's ruleset is versioned and cites every source; verify citations against current payer policy before relying on any finding for a clinical or revenue decision.

findings.description: Clinical note references at least one of the requested CPT/HCPCS codes
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-023. Heuristic: at least one CPT/HCPCS code extracted anywhere in the packet also appears inside a clinical-note document.
findings.note: No requested CPT/HCPCS code appears in any clinical-note document.
findings.remediation: Address the rule's `note` field and resubmit.

findings.description: Imaging-report document attached when the packet references imaging
findings.note: Packet references imaging but no document was classified as imaging-report.
findings.remediation: Address the rule's `note` field and resubmit.

findings.description: Step-therapy documentation present when payer requires it
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-029. Default firing condition is keyword absence; payer overlays will pre-filter on whether the requested drug class triggers step therapy.
findings.note: No step-therapy documentation anchor found. If the requested drug class requires step therapy, the packet will be denied.
findings.remediation: Address the rule's `note` field and resubmit.

findings.description: Frequency / interval present (daily, BID, q6h, etc.)
findings.note: No frequency/interval keyword (daily, BID, TID, qXh, etc.) found.
findings.remediation: Address the rule's `note` field and resubmit.

findings.description: Duration / length of approval requested is present
findings.remediation: Address the rule's `note` field and resubmit.

findings.description: Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield of New Jersey site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.20 R-PA-HORIZON-009. Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield of New Jersey's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://www.horizonblue.com/providers/prior-authorizations>
findings.note: Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield of New Jersey outpatient surgery is requested in a hospital-outpatient setting without a documented medical-necessity rationale for that site. The site-of-care policy expects an ASC / freestanding facility unless an exception is documented.
findings.remediation: Address the rule's `note` field and resubmit.

findings.description: Elective surgery: conservative-management trial documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-SURG-001. Payers approve elective surgery only after documented failure of non-operative management appropriate to the condition (e.g., joint replacement: PT + NSAIDs; spine: PT + epidurals); emergent surgery is exempt.
findings.note: Elective surgery request without a conservative-management / non-operative-trial anchor.
findings.remediation: Attach the missing surgical supporting documentation (conservative management, imaging, ASA, consent).

findings.description: Elective surgery: imaging supporting surgical indication attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-SURG-002. Surgical PAs typically require imaging (X-ray, MRI, CT, US) that demonstrates the anatomical / pathological basis for surgery; the imaging report or anchor must be in the packet.
findings.note: Surgery request without an attached imaging-report document or imaging-findings anchor.
findings.remediation: Attach the missing surgical supporting documentation (conservative management, imaging, ASA, consent).

findings.description: Surgery: ASA Physical Status classification documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-SURG-004. The ASA Physical Status is the de-facto standard for perioperative risk stratification; payers expect an ASA classification (1-5) on every elective surgery PA.
findings.note: Surgery request without an ASA Physical Status (1-5) anchor.
findings.remediation: Attach the missing surgical supporting documentation (conservative management, imaging, ASA, consent).

findings.description: Surgery: informed consent documented, dated on or before the date of surgery
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-SURG-005. Joint Commission / payer convention: a procedure-specific informed consent must be obtained before the surgical encounter. This rule narrows R-PA-059 (core consent check) to the surgical specialty trigger so the audit trail flags consent issues in the specialty section of the report.
findings.note: Surgery request without an informed-consent / surgical-consent anchor.
findings.remediation: Attach the missing surgical supporting documentation (conservative management, imaging, ASA, consent).

findings.description: Allergies / intolerances list present
Active medication list present
Packet completeness summary / cover sheet present
Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield of New Jersey prior authorization: submission channel (NaviNet / Availity / phone on member ID card) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.20 R-PA-HORIZON-003. Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield of New Jersey directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the NaviNet provider portal or Availity, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://www.horizonblue.com/providers/prior-authorizations>
findings.note: No Horizon Blue Cross Blue Shield of New Jersey submission channel (the NaviNet provider portal / Availity / EDI 278 / the phone number on the member ID card) is noted in the packet. Informational only -- does not affect medical-necessity review.

findings.description: Patient name present in at least one document
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-001 (core, payer-agnostic).
findings.evidence: Found "Mary C Roe" in horizon-pa-form.txt

findings.description: Date of birth present in at least one document
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-002 (core, payer-agnostic).
findings.evidence: DOB "1968-11-22" in horizon-pa-form.txt

findings.description: Member ID present in at least one document
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-003. Payer-specific format validation lives in payer overlays (v52-2+).
findings.evidence: Member ID "HZN123456789" in horizon-pa-form.txt

findings.description: Service date present in the packet
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-004 (core, payer-agnostic).
findings.evidence: 5 date string(s) recognized across the packet.

findings.description: Service date is not past the default retro-auth window (90 days)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-005. Default retro-auth window is 90 days; payer overlays override. Scoped to dates explicitly labeled "Date of service" / "DOS" / "Service date" so DOB strings and signature dates are excluded.
findings.evidence: No labeled service date; rule vacuously satisfied (R-PA-004 covers presence).

findings.description: Service date is not more than 365 days in the future
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-006. Hard ceiling, not payer-specific. Scoped to dates labeled "Date of service" / "DOS" / "Service date".
findings.evidence: Furthest labeled service date is 27 days in the future.

findings.description: At least one CPT or HCPCS code present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-007 (core, payer-agnostic). AMA CPT / CMS HCPCS Level II.
findings.evidence: Codes: 29881

findings.description: Each extracted CPT code is well-formed (5 digits) and not on the bundled deleted-codes list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-008. v52-1k ships an empty deleted-codes table; the rule is a format-strict pass and expands as payer-overlay waves populate the table per §5.3.
findings.evidence: 1 code(s) format-valid; none on the bundled deleted list.

findings.description: Each extracted HCPCS code is well-formed (letter + 4 digits) and not on the bundled deleted-codes list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-009. v52-1k ships an empty deleted-codes table.
findings.evidence: No HCPCS Level II code; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: At least one ICD-10-CM diagnosis code present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-010 (core, payer-agnostic). ICD-10-CM, public domain (CMS / CDC).
findings.evidence: Codes: M23.205

findings.description: Each extracted ICD-10-CM code is well-formed and not on the bundled deleted-codes list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-011. v52-1k ships an empty deleted-codes table; CMS ICD-10-CM annual updates populate it via the maintainer refresh script.
findings.evidence: 1 code(s) format-valid; none on the bundled deleted list.

findings.description: No bundled NCCI procedure-to-procedure (PTP) edit-pair conflict among the extracted CPT codes
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-012. v52-1k ships an empty NCCI PTP-pairs table per §5.3; populated to ~5,000 high-volume pairs by a later wave.
findings.evidence: Fewer than 2 distinct CPT codes; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Place-of-service code is present and on the bundled CMS list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-013. CMS Place of Service codes (POS list, public).
findings.evidence: POS code(s): 22

findings.description: Each CPT modifier present is a well-formed 2-character code
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-014. Wave 52-1j enforces format only; payer-specific permissibility lands with payer overlays.
findings.evidence: No CPT modifier suffix found; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Quantity / units present and >= 1
findings.evidence: Quantity 1 found.

findings.description: Ordering provider NPI passes Luhn checksum
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-016. NPPES Luhn-mod-10 with the 80840 issuer prefix. Registry membership is NOT verified (Sophie does not ship the NPPES dump).

findings.description: Ordering provider signature present in at least one document
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-017. Detects "Signature:" / "Signed by" / "Electronically signed" / "/s/" anchors.
findings.evidence: Signature anchor in horizon-pa-form.txt

findings.description: Ordering provider signature is dated (only if a signature is present)
findings.evidence: Dated signature in horizon-pa-form.txt

findings.description: Servicing facility / second NPI present (distinct from ordering)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-019. Most institutional PA packets carry both an ordering and a servicing NPI; v52-1h flags when only one Luhn-valid NPI is in the packet.
findings.evidence: 2 distinct Luhn-valid NPIs found.

findings.description: Servicing facility TIN present (9 digits)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-020. Federal tax ID expected on most institutional PA packets.

findings.description: Clinical-note content appears in at least one document
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-021. Keyword anchors: "chief complaint", "history of present illness", "assessment and plan", "progress note", "clinical note", "soap note".
findings.evidence: Clinical-note anchor in horizon-note.txt

findings.description: Clinical-note document has at least one extractable date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-022. Default clinical-look-back window is payer-specific; v52-1h checks date presence inside the clinical-note document(s) only.
findings.evidence: Date(s) found in clinical-note: horizon-note.txt

findings.description: Medical-necessity statement present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-024. Free-text anchor: "medical necessity", "medically necessary", "necessary because", "necessary due to".
findings.evidence: Anchor in horizon-pa-form.txt

findings.description: Lab-result document attached when the packet references labs
findings.evidence: No lab references in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Each attached lab-result document is dated within the default freshness window (360 days)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-027. Default freshness window is 12 months; payer overlays override.
findings.evidence: No lab-result documents to evaluate.

findings.description: Each attached imaging-report document is dated within the default freshness window (12 months)
findings.evidence: No imaging-report documents to evaluate.

findings.description: Prior-treatment list present when step therapy applies
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-030. Triggered when a step-therapy anchor is present; passes when the packet documents at least one prior tried-and-failed medication.
findings.evidence: No step-therapy anchor; rule vacuously satisfied (R-PA-029 covers presence).

findings.description: Height / weight present when the packet references a weight-based dose
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-033. Triggered by "mg/kg" / "weight-based" anchors in the packet text.
findings.evidence: No weight-based-dose anchor; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Renal function present when the packet references a renally-dosed agent
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-034. Triggered by "renal dose adjustment" / "CrCl" / common renally-dosed drug names.
findings.evidence: No renally-dosed-agent anchor; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Hepatic function (LFTs) present when packet references a hepatically-dosed agent
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-035. Triggered by "hepatic dose adjustment" / common hepatically-cleared drug names.
findings.evidence: No hepatically-dosed-agent anchor; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Submission is a resubmission iff a prior-auth-denial document is attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-038. The packet declares resubmission/reconsideration intent and a prior-auth-denial document is attached, or neither is the case.
findings.evidence: Not a resubmission; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Resubmission references the original PA reference number
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-039. Only checked when a resubmission/denial signal is present.
findings.evidence: Not a resubmission; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Resubmission addresses each reason cited in the prior denial
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-040. Heuristic: a "denial reason" or "response to denial" anchor is present when a prior-denial document is in the packet.
findings.evidence: No prior-auth-denial document; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: At-risk PHI (SSN) is NOT present in the packet
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-041. SSA SSN format. SSN in a PA packet is a PHI minimization concern; payers rarely require it.
findings.evidence: No SSN-shaped string found.

findings.description: Each PDF document in the packet is text-extractable (non-zero extracted text length)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-042. A scanned PDF without an embedded text layer fails this rule; the user is steered toward OCR before resubmission (spec-v52 §2).
findings.evidence: No PDF documents in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: No document in the packet is password-protected or encrypted
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-043. Triggered by the view's parse-error metadata (encrypted PDFs and password-protected DOCXs throw at extract time) and by explicit "password protected" / "encrypted document" anchors in the extracted text.
findings.evidence: 2 document(s) opened without an encryption error.

findings.description: Every document in the packet has extractable content (non-zero text length)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-044. Catches password-protected, corrupted, or empty documents that the ingest step could not open.
findings.evidence: 2 document(s) opened cleanly.

findings.description: Total packet size <= 50 MB (default payer max)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-045. Default payer maximum is 50 MB; payer overlays may raise / lower.
findings.evidence: Packet is 0 MB.

findings.description: No corrupted character encoding (mojibake) in extracted text
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-046. Non-zero count of U+FFFD REPLACEMENT CHARACTER signals a non-UTF8 source that the parser fell back on.
findings.evidence: No U+FFFD replacement chars in extracted text.

findings.description: Patient address present (info — only fires when a payer overlay requires it)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-047. Default payer-agnostic posture: vacuously satisfied at v52-1j. Payer overlays in v52-2+ flip this to "required" per plan.
findings.evidence: No payer overlay loaded; rule vacuously satisfied until v52-2+.

findings.description: Subscriber relationship to patient indicated (info — payer-overlay-gated)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-048. Default payer-agnostic posture: vacuously satisfied at v52-1j.
findings.evidence: No payer overlay loaded; rule vacuously satisfied until v52-2+.

findings.description: Other insurance / COB information indicated (info — payer-overlay-gated)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-049. Default payer-agnostic posture: vacuously satisfied at v52-1j.
findings.evidence: No payer overlay loaded; rule vacuously satisfied until v52-2+.

findings.description: Diagnosis-procedure linkage shown (an ICD-10 code and a CPT/HCPCS code co-occur within the same document)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-050. Heuristic: at least one document carries both an ICD-10 code and a CPT/HCPCS code within ~400 characters of each other.
findings.evidence: ICD-10 + CPT/HCPCS co-occur in horizon-pa-form.txt

findings.description: Procedure description matches the CPT short descriptor (info — descriptor table not shipped at v52-1j)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-051. AMA CPT short descriptors are copyrighted; per spec-v52 §5.3, descriptor-match degrades to an info-level "check skipped" finding until a license-clean source ships.
findings.evidence: CPT descriptor table not loaded (license-gated); descriptor match skipped.

findings.description: Date of injury present when an external-cause ICD-10 code (V/W/X/Y) is in the packet
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-052. ICD-10-CM external-cause codes (V00-Y99) signal injury-related encounters; payers require a date of injury.
findings.evidence: No external-cause (V/W/X/Y) ICD-10 code; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Authorization not requested for a code on the bundled no-PA-needed list (v52-1f: empty; expands with payer overlays)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-053. Wave 52-1f ships an empty bundled no-PA list; the rule remains in the table so payer overlays can populate the list without an engine change.
findings.evidence: No bundled no-PA-needed list at v52-1f close.

findings.description: Each modifier 25 / 59 is accompanied by supporting documentation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-054. Modifiers 25 and 59 are the two most-audited CPT modifiers; both require documentation of a separately identifiable service.
findings.evidence: No modifier 25 / 59 anchor; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Bilateral procedure flag matches modifier 50 presence
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-055. "Bilateral" mentioned in the clinical narrative should be accompanied by modifier 50 on the CPT line.
findings.evidence: No bilateral mention; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anesthesia time is documented when an anesthesia CPT is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-056. Anesthesia CPT range is 00100-01999; anesthesia claims require start/stop times.
findings.evidence: No anesthesia CPT or anesthesia anchor; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Assistant surgeon modifier (80/81/82/AS) is accompanied by a second Luhn-valid NPI
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-057. Modifiers 80/81/82 (assistant surgeon, MD) and AS (PA/NP assistant) require the assistant to be identifiable; payers expect a second NPI.
findings.evidence: No assistant-surgeon anchor; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Unlisted-code procedure has a narrative justification
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-058. CPT unlisted codes require a clinician narrative explaining what was done.
findings.evidence: No unlisted-procedure anchor; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Date of consent (when a consent document is present) is on or before the date of service
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.1 R-PA-059. Consent must be obtained before the procedure.
findings.evidence: No consent document; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna precertification: medical-necessity criteria (Clinical Policy Bulletin / CMS / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-001. Aetna determines coverage from "plan documents and (when applicable) a review of clinical information to determine whether clinical guidelines/criteria for coverage are met," applying Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletins, CMS guidelines, or MCG criteria, and asks providers to review the applicable CPB before submitting. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna precertification: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-002. Aetna instructs providers to submit medical records related to the member's condition for which treatment is proposed; a precertification with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna precertification: submission channel (EDI / secure provider portal / phone on member ID card) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-003. Aetna: "You can submit a precertification by electronic data interchange (EDI), through our secure provider website or by phone, using the number on the member's ID card." Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna precertification: requested service is on Aetna's participating-provider precertification list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-004. Aetna precertification applies only to services on its "Participating provider precertification list"; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-7a ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna precertification: procedure-specific clinical questionnaire / precertification information request form completed when required
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-005. Aetna: "Providers may be asked to complete a short questionnaire to provide additional clinical information," and publishes procedure-specific Precertification Information Request Forms (e.g. spinal surgery, obesity/bariatric surgery, dorsal column / spinal-cord stimulator). When the packet requests one of those procedures, the matching questionnaire response should be present. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna inpatient precertification: concurrent-review documentation (clinical progress + discharge / continuing-care plan) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-006. Aetna concurrent review "collect[s] information from the care team about the patient's condition and progress to determine coverage" and develops a discharge and continuing-care plan early in the stay; an inpatient request should carry that clinical update. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/utilization-management/concurrent-review.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna site-of-service review: hospital-outpatient MRI / CT addresses the site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-007. Aetna: "Some members will have site-of-care requirements for MRI and CT scans when services are requested in a hospital outpatient setting." A hospital-outpatient advanced-imaging request should document why that site is required rather than a freestanding center. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna expedited / urgent precertification: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-008. Aetna processes a request on an expedited basis when "a delay in decision making might seriously jeopardize the life or health of the member or [the] ability to regain maximum function." A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna precertification: objective evidence (visual field / photographs / measurements) present for procedures whose CPB requires it
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-009. Several Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletins condition coverage on objective evidence -- e.g. visual-field tests and margin-reflex-distance photographs for blepharoplasty, photographs and excised-tissue weight for panniculectomy / reduction mammaplasty. When the packet requests one of those procedures, that objective evidence should be attached. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna precertification: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-010. Aetna's precertification information request for physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs asks for the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code, so the request maps to a specific product and dose. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna drug precertification: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Aetna asks providers to "list all medications the patient has tried specific to the diagnosis and specify therapeutic failure, including length of therapy for each drug." (Step therapy does not apply if the requested drug was already used within the past 365 days.) <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/medicare/part-b-drug-um.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna bariatric surgery (CPB 0157): BMI and a structured / supervised weight-management program documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-012. Aetna CPB 0157 (Obesity Surgery) requires class III obesity (BMI >= 40, or >= 35 with a serious comorbidity) and documentation of a structured / physician-supervised weight-management program before bariatric surgery. <https://www.aetna.com/cpb/medical/data/100_199/0157.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna genetic testing (CPB 0140): pre-test genetic counseling and personal / family history documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-013. Aetna CPB 0140 conditions many genetic tests on "pre- and post-test counseling by an appropriate independent provider (not an employee of the genetics testing laboratory)" and a supporting personal / family history (pedigree). <https://www.aetna.com/cpb/medical/data/100_199/0140.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-014. Aetna accepts retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna Outpatient Surgical Procedures policy: hospital-setting elective surgery documents the medical-necessity rationale for the site
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-015. Aetna's Outpatient Surgical Procedures policy: elective procedures in the program "should be performed in an ambulatory surgical center (ASC) or office setting unless the medical necessity criteria ... is met," and "clinical rationale and documentation must be provided for ... exceptions." A hospital-outpatient / inpatient elective surgery should document why the higher-acuity site is required. Informational. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/outpatient-surgical-procedures.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna DME / home-health precertification: a signed, dated written order accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-016. Aetna precertification for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna transplant precertification: routed through the National Medical Excellence / Institutes of Excellence program with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-017. Aetna directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through its National Medical Excellence Program (NME) / Institutes of Excellence (IOE) network; a transplant precertification should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented CPB exception is present when the service is flagged experimental
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-018. Aetna excludes services it deems experimental or investigational under the applicable Clinical Policy Bulletin; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the CPB allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna reconsideration / appeal: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-019. When a precertification is resubmitted as a reconsideration or appeal, Aetna asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Aetna out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.7 R-PA-AETNA-020. Aetna reviews out-of-network precertification for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.aetna.com/health-care-professionals/precertification.html>
findings.evidence: Not an Aetna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem prior authorization: medical-necessity criteria (Clinical UM Guideline / Medical Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-001. Anthem adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Clinical UM Guidelines and Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/policies/clinical-guidelines/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-002. Anthem requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem prior authorization: submission channel (Availity / Interactive Care Reviewer / phone on member ID card) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-003. Anthem directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the Availity Essentials portal's Interactive Care Reviewer (ICR), by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem prior authorization: requested service is on Anthem's prior-authorization requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-004. Anthem prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization requirement lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-9 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the Aetna / UHC -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-005. Anthem requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-006. Anthem requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for the Carelon (AIM) imaging program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-007. Anthem delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to Carelon Medical Benefits Management (formerly AIM Specialty Health); the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-008. Anthem processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-009. Anthem's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/policies/clinical-guidelines/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-010. Anthem / CarelonRx review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Anthem / CarelonRx requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/policies/clinical-guidelines/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for the Carelon lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-012. Anthem delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to Carelon Medical Benefits Management, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/policies/clinical-guidelines/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Clinical Criteria
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-013. Anthem specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Clinical Criteria (administered with CarelonRx) that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/policies/clinical-guidelines/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-014. Anthem considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-015. Anthem prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/prior-authorization/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-016. Anthem behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://www.anthem.com/provider/policies/clinical-guidelines/>
findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Anthem transplant prior authorization: routed through the Anthem transplant network / Blue Distinction Centers with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.9 R-PA-ANTHEM-017. findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.evidence: Not an Anthem packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

When a prior authorization is resubmitted as a reconsideration or appeal, Anthem asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-009. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-010. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-012. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-013. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-014. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-015. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-016. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama transplant prior authorization: routed through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-017. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant network; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-018. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama reconsideration / appeal: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as a reconsideration or appeal, Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.23 R-PA-BCBSAL-020. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama reviews out-of-network prior authorization for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.bcbsal.org/web/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana prior authorization: coverage criteria (Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana Medical Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.28 R-PA-BCBSLA-001. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://www.bcbsla.com/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.28 R-PA-BCBSLA-002. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://www.bcbsla.com/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana prior authorization: submission channel (iLinkBlue / Availity / phone on member ID card) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.28 R-PA-BCBSLA-003. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the iLinkBlue provider portal or Availity, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://www.bcbsla.com/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana prior authorization: requested service is on Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.28 R-PA-BCBSLA-004. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-28 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the Aetna / UHC / Anthem / Cigna / Humana / HCSC / Highmark / Florida Blue / BCBSM / Blue Shield of California / Independence Blue Cross / CareFirst / Blue Cross NC / Horizon / BCBS Tennessee / BCBS Massachusetts / BCBS Alabama / BCBS South Carolina / Arkansas BCBS / BCBS Kansas City / BCBS Minnesota -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.bcbsla.com/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.28 R-PA-BCBSLA-005. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://www.bcbsla.com/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.28 R-PA-BCBSLA-006. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://www.bcbsla.com/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana's advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.28 R-PA-BCBSLA-007. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://www.bcbsla.com/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Louisiana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-002. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts prior authorization: submission channel (Provider Central / Availity / phone on member ID card) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-003. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts Provider Central portal or Availity, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts prior authorization: requested service is on Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-004. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-22 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the Aetna / UHC / Anthem / Cigna / Humana / HCSC / Highmark / Florida Blue / BCBSM / Blue Shield of California / Independence Blue Cross / CareFirst / Blue Cross NC / Horizon / BCBS Tennessee -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-005. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-006. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts's advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-007. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-008. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-009. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-010. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-012. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-013. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-014. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-015. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-016. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts transplant prior authorization: routed through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-017. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant network; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.22 R-PA-BCBSMA-018. Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://provider.bluecrossma.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts reconsideration / appeal: references the original determination and adds new clinical information

findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented

findings.description: Blue Cross Blue Shield of South Carolina prior authorization: requested service is on Blue Cross Blue Shield of South Carolina's prior-authorization / notification requirement list

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City prior authorization: submission channel (Availity Essentials / Blue KC provider portal / phone on member ID card) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-003. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the Availity Essentials portal or the Blue KC provider portal, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City prior authorization: requested service is on Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-004. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-26 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the Aetna / UHC / Anthem / Cigna / Humana / HCSC / Highmark / Florida Blue / BCBSM / Blue Shield of California / Independence Blue Cross / CareFirst / Blue Cross NC / Horizon / BCBS Tennessee / BCBS Massachusetts / BCBS Alabama / BCBS South Carolina / Arkansas BCBS -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-005. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-006. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City's advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-007. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-008. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-009. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-010. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-012. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-013. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-014. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-015. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-016. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City transplant prior authorization: routed through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-017. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant network; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.26 R-PA-BLUEKC-018. Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://providers.bluekc.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City reconsideration / appeal: references the original determination and adds new clinical information

findings.evidence: Not a Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
