findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.18 R-PA-CAREFIRST-015. CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://provider.carefirst.com/providers/preauth-precert.page>
findings.evidence: Not a CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.18 R-PA-CAREFIRST-016. CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://provider.carefirst.com/providers/preauth-precert.page>
findings.evidence: Not a CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield transplant prior authorization: routed through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.18 R-PA-CAREFIRST-017. CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant network; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://provider.carefirst.com/providers/preauth-precert.page>
findings.evidence: Not a CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.18 R-PA-CAREFIRST-018. CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://provider.carefirst.com/providers/preauth-precert.page>
findings.evidence: Not a CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield reconsideration / appeal: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.18 R-PA-CAREFIRST-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as a reconsideration or appeal, CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://provider.carefirst.com/providers/preauth-precert.page>
findings.evidence: Not a CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.18 R-PA-CAREFIRST-020. CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield reviews out-of-network prior authorization for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://provider.carefirst.com/providers/preauth-precert.page>
findings.evidence: Not a CareFirst BlueCross BlueShield packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna prior authorization: coverage criteria (Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-001. Cigna adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Coverage Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-002. Cigna requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna prior authorization: submission channel (CignaforHCP provider portal / Availity / phone on member ID card) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-003. Cigna directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the CignaforHCP provider portal, the Availity Essentials portal, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna prior authorization: requested service is on Cigna's precertification / prior-authorization requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-004. Cigna precertification applies only to services on its published prior-authorization requirement lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-10 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the Aetna / UHC / Anthem -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-005. Cigna requires precertification to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-006. Cigna requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for the eviCore imaging program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-007. Cigna delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to eviCore by Evernorth; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-008. Cigna processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-009. Cigna's site-of-care medical coverage policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-010. Cigna / Accredo review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Cigna / Express Scripts requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for the eviCore lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-012. Cigna delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to eviCore by Evernorth, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna specialty / injectable drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Coverage Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-013. Cigna specialty and injectable drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Coverage Policies (administered with Accredo / Express Scripts) that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.10 R-PA-CIGNA-014. Cigna considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Cigna transplant prior authorization: routed through the Cigna LifeSOURCE Transplant Network / Centers of Excellence with a transplant-center evaluation

findings.evidence: Not a Cigna packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS DME: face-to-face encounter documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-001. CMS NCD-280.x; Medicare requires a face-to-face encounter with the prescribing physician within 6 months prior to the written order for most DME categories.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS DME: Detailed / Standard Written Order (DWO/SWO) present and dated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-002. CMS IOM Pub 100-08 ch. 5; a Standard Written Order (or pre-2020 Detailed Written Order) signed and dated by the prescribing physician is required before DME delivery.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS DME: Standard Written Order elements complete (beneficiary, item, date, quantity, prescriber NPI, signature)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-003. CMS IOM Pub 100-08 ch. 5; the SWO must carry the beneficiary identity, item description / HCPCS, order date, quantity, treating-practitioner NPI, and a dated prescriber signature.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS DME: proof of delivery requirements documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-004. CMS IOM Pub 100-08 ch. 4 §4.26; supplier must retain proof of delivery (POD) including date and recipient signature.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS mobility device: patient functional status documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-005. CMS LCD L33788 (Power Mobility Devices) requires a documented mobility limitation evaluation: ADL impact, ambulation distance, balance, and home accessibility.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS PAP device: sleep study results documented within 12 months prior to initial PAP order
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-006. LCD L33718 (Positive Airway Pressure Devices) requires a Medicare-covered sleep test with results within 12 months prior to initiating PAP therapy.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS PAP continuation: 90-day adherence / compliance documentation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-007. LCD L33718 requires documentation that the beneficiary is using PAP >= 4 hours/night on >= 70% of nights during a consecutive 30-day period within the first 90 days of initial PAP use, before continued coverage past three months.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS home oxygen: qualifying arterial blood gas or pulse-oximetry result documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-008. CMS NCD 240.2 / LCD L33797 (Oxygen and Oxygen Equipment) requires a qualifying ABG (PaO2 <= 55 mmHg) or pulse oximetry (SpO2 <= 88%) on room air at rest, during sleep, or with exertion.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS DME: supplier PTAN documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-009. CMS supplier PTAN (Provider Transaction Access Number) is required on DME claims so the MAC can match the supplier to the Medicare enrollment record.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS hospital bed: medical-necessity documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-011. LCD L33820 (Hospital Beds and Accessories) requires documentation that the beneficiary has a condition requiring positioning of the body in ways not feasible with an ordinary bed (e.g., head elevation > 30°, frequent body position change).
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS enteral nutrition: inability to ingest / projected duration documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-012. LCD L33783 (Enteral Nutrition) requires documentation of a permanent (>= 90 days) inability to take nutrition orally and a functioning gastrointestinal tract.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS nebulizer: obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-013. LCD L33370 (Nebulizers) requires documentation of a covered diagnosis (COPD, asthma, cystic fibrosis, bronchiectasis, etc.) and the inhalation drug being delivered.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS TENS unit: chronic intractable pain > 3 months + failed conventional therapy documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-014. NCD 160.13 / LCD L33802 (TENS) requires chronic intractable pain present > 3 months and documentation of failure or unsuitability of conventional therapy.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS NPWT: wound type / size / failed standard wound care documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-015. LCD L33821 (Negative Pressure Wound Therapy) requires documentation of a covered wound type (Stage III/IV pressure ulcer, neuropathic ulcer, etc.) plus failure of standard wound care.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS lower-limb prosthesis: K-level / functional rehabilitation potential documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-016. LCD L33787 (Lower Limb Prostheses) requires documentation of the beneficiary's functional level (K0-K4) and rehabilitation potential.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS orthotics: documented condition + L-code present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-017. LCD L33686 (Ankle-Foot / Knee-Ankle-Foot Orthoses) requires documentation of a condition (e.g., drop foot, peroneal palsy, post-CVA) plus an appropriate L-code HCPCS.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS continuous glucose monitor: insulin therapy + frequent self-monitoring documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-018. LCD L33822 (Glucose Monitors) requires documentation of insulin therapy AND a history of frequent self-monitoring (>= 4 fingersticks/day) or problematic hypoglycemia before CGM coverage.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS post-transplant immunosuppressives: date and Medicare-covered transplant type documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-019. CMS Part B covers immunosuppressive drugs for beneficiaries who had a Medicare-covered organ transplant (kidney, heart, liver, lung, pancreas, intestinal). The transplant date and organ must be documented.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS parenteral nutrition: documented GI tract failure + caloric requirements
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-020. LCD L33799 (Parenteral Nutrition) requires documentation that the GI tract is non-functional or inaccessible (e.g., short bowel syndrome, motility disorder, fistula) and an estimate of caloric / protein requirements.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS lymphedema pneumatic compression pump: documented lymphedema + failed conservative therapy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-021. LCD L33829 (Pneumatic Compression Devices) requires documentation of chronic venous insufficiency or lymphedema plus failure of a 4-week trial of conservative therapy (compression garments / manual lymphatic drainage).
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS external infusion pump: covered chronic-disease indication + drug compatibility documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-022. LCD L33794 (External Infusion Pumps) requires a covered indication (e.g., chemotherapy, antifungal therapy, parenteral hydration, inotropic therapy) and an FDA-approved infusion drug.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS ostomy supplies: ostomy type documented + quantity within monthly utilization limits
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-023. LCD L33828 (Ostomy Supplies) requires documentation of ostomy type (colostomy, ileostomy, urostomy) and quantity must align with the LCD utilization parameters.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS urinary catheters: permanent urinary incontinence / retention diagnosis documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-024. LCD L33803 (Urological Supplies) requires documentation of permanent urinary incontinence or urinary retention.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS surgical dressings: wound surface area + dressing-change frequency documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-025. LCD L33831 (Surgical Dressings) requires documentation of wound surface area and frequency of dressing changes so utilization can be matched to the dressing category.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS post-cataract refractive lenses: cataract-surgery date + surgical CPT documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2 R-PA-CMS-026. NCD 80.4 covers one pair of conventional eyeglasses or contact lenses after each cataract surgery with insertion of an intraocular lens; the surgery date and procedure code must be documented.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare FFS; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Florida Blue pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for Florida Blue's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-012. Florida Blue delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-013. Florida Blue specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-014. Florida Blue considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-015. Florida Blue prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-016. Florida Blue behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue transplant prior authorization: routed through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-017. Florida Blue directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant network; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-018. Florida Blue excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue reconsideration / appeal: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as a reconsideration or appeal, Florida Blue asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Florida Blue out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.14 R-PA-FLBLUE-020. Florida Blue reviews out-of-network prior authorization for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.floridablue.com/providers/tools-resources/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Florida Blue packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Genetic testing: family history of relevant condition documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-GEN-001. Payer medical policies for hereditary-cancer / cardiomyopathy / Lynch / BRCA panels require a documented family history meeting test-specific criteria (e.g., NCCN BRCA testing criteria for hereditary breast / ovarian).
findings.evidence: No genetic-testing CPT (81xxx) in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Genetic testing: pre-test / post-test genetic counseling documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-GEN-002. ACMG / NSGC guidelines and most payer policies require documented pre-test (and post-test for positive results) genetic counseling by a board-certified genetic counselor or qualified clinician before a hereditary-disorder panel is ordered.
findings.evidence: No genetic-testing CPT in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Genetic testing: test specificity / panel scope matches the documented clinical indication
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-GEN-003. Payers deny broad-panel testing when a more targeted single-gene or focused panel matches the documented indication; the packet should state why the chosen panel scope (single-gene / focused / comprehensive / WES / WGS) is appropriate.
findings.evidence: No genetic-testing CPT in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Genetic testing: personal clinical indication documented (symptoms / dx / risk profile)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-GEN-004. Predictive / asymptomatic genetic testing is approved only with documented risk based on personal history (signs / symptoms / preexisting dx / age criteria) or qualifying family history (R-PA-GEN-001) -- the packet must carry one or both.
findings.evidence: No genetic-testing CPT in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Genetic testing: informed consent specific to genetic testing (covers GINA / insurance / employment implications)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-GEN-005. Many state laws and payer policies require an informed consent specific to genetic testing -- separate from general procedure consent -- that addresses Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act (GINA) protections, incidental findings, and family implications.
findings.evidence: No genetic-testing CPT in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HCSC prior authorization: coverage criteria (HCSC Medical Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.12 R-PA-HCSC-001. HCSC adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://www.bcbsil.com/provider/clinical/clinical-resources/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not an HCSC packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HCSC prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached

findings.evidence: Not an HCSC packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HCSC prior authorization: submission channel (Availity Essentials / phone on member ID card) indicated

findings.evidence: Not an HCSC packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HCSC prior authorization: requested service is on HCSC's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.12 R-PA-HCSC-004. HCSC prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-12 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the Aetna / UHC / Anthem / Cigna / Humana -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.bcbsil.com/provider/clinical/clinical-resources/prior-authorization>
findings.evidence: Not an HCSC packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) prior authorization: requested service is on HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii)'s prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-004. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-29 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the Aetna / UHC / Anthem / Cigna / Humana / HCSC / Highmark / Florida Blue / BCBSM / Blue Shield of California / Independence Blue Cross / CareFirst / Blue Cross NC / Horizon / BCBS Tennessee / BCBS Massachusetts / BCBS Alabama / BCBS South Carolina / Arkansas BCBS / BCBS Kansas City / BCBS Minnesota / BCBS Louisiana -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-005. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-006. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii)'s advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-007. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-008. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-009. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii)'s site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-010. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-011. For drugs subject to step therapy HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii)'s genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-012. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-013. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-014. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-015. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-016. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) transplant prior authorization: routed through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-017. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant network; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-018. HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://hmsa.com/providers/>
findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) reconsideration / appeal: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.29 R-PA-HMSA-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as a reconsideration or appeal, HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. findings.evidence: Not a HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: HMSA (Blue Cross Blue Shield of Hawaii) out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented

findings.description: Humana behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.11 R-PA-HUMANA-016. Humana behavioral-health prior authorization (administered by Humana Behavioral Health) reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://www.humana.com/provider/medical-resources/authorizations-referrals>
findings.evidence: Not a Humana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Humana transplant prior authorization: routed through the Humana National Transplant Network / Centers of Excellence with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.11 R-PA-HUMANA-017. Humana directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through the Humana National Transplant Network / Centers of Excellence; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.humana.com/provider/medical-resources/authorizations-referrals>
findings.evidence: Not a Humana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Humana experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.11 R-PA-HUMANA-018. Humana excludes services its Medical Coverage Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.humana.com/provider/medical-resources/authorizations-referrals>
findings.evidence: Not a Humana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Humana reconsideration / appeal: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.11 R-PA-HUMANA-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as a reconsideration or appeal, Humana asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.humana.com/provider/medical-resources/authorizations-referrals>
findings.evidence: Not a Humana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Humana out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.11 R-PA-HUMANA-020. Humana reviews out-of-network prior authorization for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.humana.com/provider/medical-resources/authorizations-referrals>
findings.evidence: Not a Humana packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross prior authorization: coverage criteria (Independence Blue Cross Medical Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-001. Independence Blue Cross adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-002. Independence Blue Cross requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross prior authorization: submission channel (Availity Essentials / Provider Resource Center / phone on member ID card) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-003. Independence Blue Cross directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the Availity Essentials portal or the Provider Resource Center, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross prior authorization: requested service is on Independence Blue Cross's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-004. Independence Blue Cross prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-17 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the Aetna / UHC / Anthem / Cigna / Humana / HCSC / Highmark / Florida Blue / BCBSM / Blue Shield of California -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-005. Independence Blue Cross requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-006. Independence Blue Cross requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for Independence Blue Cross's advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-007. Independence Blue Cross delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-008. Independence Blue Cross processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-009. Independence Blue Cross's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-010. Independence Blue Cross review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Independence Blue Cross pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for Independence Blue Cross's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-012. Independence Blue Cross delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-013. Independence Blue Cross specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-014. Independence Blue Cross considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-015. Independence Blue Cross prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross transplant prior authorization: routed through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-017. Independence Blue Cross directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through the Blue Distinction Centers for Transplant network; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-018. Independence Blue Cross excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as a reconsideration or appeal, Independence Blue Cross asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Independence Blue Cross out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.17 R-PA-IBX-020. Independence Blue Cross reviews out-of-network prior authorization for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.ibx.com/resources/for-providers/authorizations>
findings.evidence: Not a Independence Blue Cross packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Infusion / specialty drug: J-code present + NDC documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-INF-001. Cross-payer convention: physician-administered drugs require the J-code AND the 11-digit NDC so the manufacturer rebate and drug-specific edits can be applied (echoes R-PA-MCD-006 for Medicaid; this rule generalizes).
findings.evidence: No J-code in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Infusion / specialty drug: weight-based dose calculation shown when dosing is per kg
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-INF-002. Weight-based biologic / chemotherapy infusions require an explicit weight + dose calculation so the payer can verify the dose against FDA labeling.
findings.evidence: No J-code in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Infusion / specialty drug: site-of-care indicator (home / clinic / hospital infusion) documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-INF-003. Payers route specialty-drug PAs to different review queues based on site of care (home infusion has different rules than hospital outpatient); the packet must label the planned site.
findings.evidence: No J-code in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Infusion / specialty drug: FDA-approved indication referenced for the diagnosis
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-INF-004. Biologic / specialty-drug PA approval typically requires that the requested drug have an FDA-approved indication matching the documented diagnosis (or, for off-label use, an NCCN / compendia citation).
findings.evidence: No J-code in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Infusion / specialty drug: premedication / infusion-reaction monitoring plan documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-INF-005. Many biologics carry an infusion-reaction risk (rituximab, infliximab, IV iron, etc.); the packet should reference a premedication regimen or in-suite monitoring plan when the drug carries that risk.
findings.evidence: No J-code in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage HMO: PCP referral present for specialist services
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-001. MA HMO and HMO-POS plans require a PCP referral before non-emergent specialist services unless the member has an open-access benefit.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage: in-network status confirmed OR out-of-network exception documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-002. MA plans pay differently in vs. out of network; the packet should indicate the servicing provider's network status, or an OON exception (continuity-of-care, urgent / emergent, network inadequacy) when going OON.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage gatekeepered plan: servicing specialist NPI distinct from ordering PCP NPI
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-003. On a gatekeepered MA plan, the specialist NPI on the PA must differ from the ordering PCP NPI; both must be Luhn-valid and present in the packet.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage: plan name + member-ID format present on the packet
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-004. MA plans require the plan name on the request form and a member ID matching the plan's issuer-specific format; without both the payer cannot route the PA to the correct plan unit.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage: service location is within the plan's service area
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-005. MA plans cover specific geographies; an out-of-area service location triggers different cost-share and may require an OON exception. v52-3a checks for a service-location / service-area anchor; v52-3b+ will tie this to the bundled CMS plan-service-area files when those land.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage drug request: Part B vs Part D coverage path indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-006. MA plans cover drugs under Part B (physician-administered, DME-administered, certain immunosuppressives) and Part D (most outpatient prescriptions); the packet must indicate which coverage path applies so the plan routes the PA to the correct review queue.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage D-SNP (dual-eligible Special Needs Plan): Medicaid plan information documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-007. D-SNP enrollees have both Medicare (via the MA plan) and Medicaid; coordinated benefits require the state Medicaid plan name and member ID on the PA so cost-share and coverage gaps can be resolved.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage supplemental benefit (dental / vision / hearing): plan-covered-benefit anchor documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-008. MA plans often offer supplemental dental, vision, and hearing benefits that are NOT in Original Medicare; the packet should indicate the benefit is covered under the plan's Evidence of Coverage before submission.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage Part B drug: step-therapy compliance documented when plan applies step therapy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-009. Per CMS final rule (effective 2019), MA plans may apply step therapy to Part B drugs; when step therapy applies, the prior-trial / failure documentation must be attached.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage inpatient admission: two-midnight expectation or short-stay criteria documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-010. CMS extended the two-midnight rule to MA plans effective 2024; an inpatient admission requires a documented expectation that the patient will need 2+ midnights of medically necessary hospital care, or short-stay criteria for case-by-case approval.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage: organization determination type (pre-service / concurrent / payment) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-011. CMS Chapter 13 distinguishes organization determinations by stage (pre-service before delivery, concurrent during ongoing care, payment after delivery). The packet should label which type is being requested so MA plan timeliness rules apply correctly.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage expedited review: clinical urgency / serious-jeopardy attestation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-012. MA expedited (72-hour) determinations require a treating-clinician attestation that applying the standard 14-day timeframe could seriously jeopardize the enrollee's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage transition fill: continuity-of-care anchor for new enrollee within 90 days of effective date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-013. CMS requires MA plans to honor a transition supply / continuity-of-care period (typically 90 days) for new enrollees on existing therapies; the packet should indicate the transition window when applicable.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage: hospice-election indicator (hospice services revert to Original Medicare)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-014. When an MA enrollee elects the Medicare hospice benefit, hospice-related services revert to Original Medicare; the MA plan continues to cover non-hospice services. The packet must indicate the hospice-election status when relevant so the PA routes to the correct payer.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare Advantage C-SNP / I-SNP: qualifying chronic condition or institutional status documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.3 R-PA-MA-015. Chronic-condition SNPs (C-SNPs) require documented diagnosis of a qualifying chronic condition (e.g., diabetes, CHF, ESRD, dementia); institutional SNPs (I-SNPs) require documented nursing-facility residence. The PA must reference the qualifying condition.
findings.evidence: Not Medicare Advantage; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) prior authorization: coverage criteria (Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) Medical Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.30 R-PA-MCAL-001. Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://www.medi-cal.ca.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.medi-cal.ca.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.30 R-PA-MCAL-015. Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.medi-cal.ca.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.30 R-PA-MCAL-016. Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://www.medi-cal.ca.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) transplant prior authorization: routed through a Medicaid-designated transplant center with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.30 R-PA-MCAL-017. Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through a Medicaid-designated transplant center / Center of Excellence; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.medi-cal.ca.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.30 R-PA-MCAL-018. Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.medi-cal.ca.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) appeal / reconsideration / state fair hearing: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.30 R-PA-MCAL-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as an appeal, reconsideration, or state fair hearing, Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.medi-cal.ca.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.30 R-PA-MCAL-020. Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) reviews out-of-network prior authorization for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.medi-cal.ca.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid prior authorization: coverage criteria (Arizona Medicaid Medical Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-001. Arizona Medicaid adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-002. Arizona Medicaid requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid prior authorization: submission channel (AHCCCS Online / phone) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-003. Arizona Medicaid directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the AHCCCS Online provider portal, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid prior authorization: requested service is on Arizona Medicaid's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-004. Arizona Medicaid prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-43 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the commercial overlay (Aetna through HMSA) and the California / New York / Texas / Florida / Ohio / Illinois / Washington / Georgia / North Carolina / Pennsylvania / Michigan / New Jersey Medicaid -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-005. Arizona Medicaid requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-006. Arizona Medicaid requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for Arizona Medicaid's advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-007. Arizona Medicaid delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-008. Arizona Medicaid processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-009. Arizona Medicaid's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-010. Arizona Medicaid review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Arizona Medicaid pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for Arizona Medicaid's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-012. Arizona Medicaid delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-013. Arizona Medicaid specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Arizona Medicaid retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.43 R-PA-MCAZ-014. Arizona Medicaid considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.
