An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.azahcccs.gov/>
findings.evidence: Not a Arizona Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid: member-ID line present (state Medicaid CIN / recipient ID)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-001. All state Medicaid programs require a state-issued recipient / CIN / RID on the PA request; without it the state cannot resolve eligibility.
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid pediatric patient: EPSDT documentation present when applicable
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-002. EPSDT (Early and Periodic Screening, Diagnostic, and Treatment) is mandatory under federal Medicaid for beneficiaries < 21; pediatric PA packets should reference EPSDT screening / treatment when seeking non-routine services.
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid: eligibility-date alignment anchor for the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-003. State Medicaid programs deny PA requests when the date of service falls outside the recipient's eligibility span; the packet should indicate the eligibility window (start / end / verified-as-of).
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid: medical-necessity statement aligned to state Medicaid criteria
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-004. Each state Medicaid program defines medical necessity in statute / regulation; the packet should reference the program's medical-necessity standard (federal "medically necessary" plus state-specific language).
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid: Managed Care Organization (MCO) vs FFS Medicaid indicator
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-005. ~75% of Medicaid beneficiaries are in managed care; the packet must indicate which entity (state FFS vs a contracted MCO) is the responsible payer so the PA routes to the correct review queue.
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid physician-administered drug: NDC documented alongside the J-code / HCPCS
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-006. Section 1927(a)(7) of the Social Security Act requires state Medicaid programs to collect the 11-digit NDC on physician-administered drug claims so the manufacturer rebate can be billed; the packet should carry the NDC alongside the J-code.
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid dental service: covered-benefit anchor for adult (state-optional) vs pediatric (EPSDT-mandatory)
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-007. Federal Medicaid mandates dental for children < 21 under EPSDT; adult dental coverage is state-optional and varies widely (full / emergency-only / none). The packet should indicate the coverage path.
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid non-emergency medical transportation (NEMT): trip purpose + appointment date documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-008. NEMT is a mandatory Medicaid benefit (42 CFR §431.53). PA / brokerage approvals require the trip purpose (covered medical appointment), appointment date, and origin / destination so eligibility against the trip standard can be evaluated.
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid behavioral-health service: carve-out indicator documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-009. Many state Medicaid programs carve behavioral-health benefits out to a separate plan (PIHP / BHO / specialty MCO); the packet should indicate whether BH is carved out vs integrated so the PA routes to the correct payer.
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicaid prescription drug: Medicaid Drug Rebate Program (MDRP) labeler-agreement coverage indicator
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.4 R-PA-MCD-010. Under Section 1927 of the Social Security Act, Medicaid covers outpatient prescription drugs from manufacturers that have signed a Medicaid drug-rebate agreement (Section 1927(a)). The packet should indicate the drug is from a participating manufacturer.
findings.evidence: Not Medicaid; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-006. Georgia Medicaid requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for Georgia Medicaid's advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-007. Georgia Medicaid delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-008. Georgia Medicaid processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-009. Georgia Medicaid's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-010. Georgia Medicaid review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Georgia Medicaid pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for Georgia Medicaid's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-012. Georgia Medicaid delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-013. Georgia Medicaid specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-014. Georgia Medicaid considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-015. Georgia Medicaid prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-016. Georgia Medicaid behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid transplant prior authorization: routed through a Medicaid-designated transplant center with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-017. Georgia Medicaid directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through a Medicaid-designated transplant center / Center of Excellence; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-018. Georgia Medicaid excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid appeal / reconsideration / state fair hearing: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as an appeal, reconsideration, or state fair hearing, Georgia Medicaid asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Georgia Medicaid out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.37 R-PA-MCGA-020. Georgia Medicaid reviews out-of-network prior authorization for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://medicaid.georgia.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Georgia Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Illinois Medicaid prior authorization: coverage criteria (Illinois Medicaid Medical Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.35 R-PA-MCIL-001. Illinois Medicaid adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://hfs.illinois.gov/medicalproviders.html>
findings.evidence: Not a Illinois Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Illinois Medicaid prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.35 R-PA-MCIL-002. Illinois Medicaid requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://hfs.illinois.gov/medicalproviders.html>
findings.evidence: Not a Illinois Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Illinois Medicaid prior authorization: submission channel (Illinois IMPACT / MEDI / phone) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.35 R-PA-MCIL-003. Illinois Medicaid directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the Illinois IMPACT or MEDI provider portal, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://hfs.illinois.gov/medicalproviders.html>
findings.evidence: Not a Illinois Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Illinois Medicaid prior authorization: requested service is on Illinois Medicaid's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.35 R-PA-MCIL-004. Illinois Medicaid prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-35 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the the commercial overlay (Aetna through HMSA) and the California / New York / Texas / Florida / Ohio Medicaid -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://hfs.illinois.gov/medicalproviders.html>
findings.evidence: Not a Illinois Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

Wave 52-41 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the commercial overlay (Aetna through HMSA) and the California / New York / Texas / Florida / Ohio / Illinois / Washington / Georgia / North Carolina / Pennsylvania Medicaid -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-005. Michigan Medicaid requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-006. Michigan Medicaid requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for Michigan Medicaid's advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-007. Michigan Medicaid delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-008. Michigan Medicaid processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-009. Michigan Medicaid's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-010. Michigan Medicaid review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Michigan Medicaid pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for Michigan Medicaid's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-012. Michigan Medicaid delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-013. Michigan Medicaid specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-014. Michigan Medicaid considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-015. Michigan Medicaid prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-016. Michigan Medicaid behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid transplant prior authorization: routed through a Medicaid-designated transplant center with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-017. Michigan Medicaid directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through a Medicaid-designated transplant center / Center of Excellence; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-018. Michigan Medicaid excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid appeal / reconsideration / state fair hearing: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as an appeal, reconsideration, or state fair hearing, Michigan Medicaid asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Michigan Medicaid out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.41 R-PA-MCMI-020. Michigan Medicaid reviews out-of-network prior authorization for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.michigan.gov/mdhhs/doing-business/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Michigan Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: North Carolina Medicaid prior authorization: coverage criteria (North Carolina Medicaid Medical Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.38 R-PA-MCNC-001. North Carolina Medicaid adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://medicaid.ncdhhs.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a North Carolina Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: North Carolina Medicaid prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.38 R-PA-MCNC-002. North Carolina Medicaid requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://medicaid.ncdhhs.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a North Carolina Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: North Carolina Medicaid prior authorization: submission channel (NCTracks / phone) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.38 R-PA-MCNC-003. North Carolina Medicaid directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the NCTracks provider portal, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://medicaid.ncdhhs.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a North Carolina Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: North Carolina Medicaid prior authorization: requested service is on North Carolina Medicaid's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.38 R-PA-MCNC-004. findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-001. New York State Medicaid adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-002. New York State Medicaid requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid prior authorization: submission channel (eMedNY / ePACES / phone) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-003. New York State Medicaid directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the eMedNY provider portal or ePACES, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid prior authorization: requested service is on New York State Medicaid's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-004. New York State Medicaid prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-31 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the the commercial overlay (Aetna through HMSA) and the Medi-Cal -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-005. New York State Medicaid requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-006. New York State Medicaid requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for New York State Medicaid's advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-007. New York State Medicaid delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-008. New York State Medicaid processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-009. New York State Medicaid's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-010. New York State Medicaid review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-011. For drugs subject to step therapy New York State Medicaid pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for New York State Medicaid's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-012. New York State Medicaid delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-013. New York State Medicaid specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-014. New York State Medicaid considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-015. New York State Medicaid prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-016. New York State Medicaid behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid transplant prior authorization: routed through a Medicaid-designated transplant center with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-017. New York State Medicaid directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through a Medicaid-designated transplant center / Center of Excellence; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-018. New York State Medicaid excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid appeal / reconsideration / state fair hearing: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as an appeal, reconsideration, or state fair hearing, New York State Medicaid asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.emedny.org/>
findings.evidence: Not a New York State Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: New York State Medicaid out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.31 R-PA-MCNY-020. An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.dhs.pa.gov/providers>
findings.evidence: Not a Pennsylvania Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid prior authorization: coverage criteria (Texas Medicaid Medical Policy / MCG) referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-001. Texas Medicaid adjudicates prior-authorization requests against its published Medical Policies (and MCG care guidelines); a request should cite the policy it is being judged against so the reviewer can map it to the coverage criteria. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid prior authorization: supporting medical records / clinical documentation attached
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-002. Texas Medicaid requires the clinical information that supports medical necessity to be submitted with the prior-authorization request; a request with no clinical document attached cannot be reviewed on the merits. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid prior authorization: submission channel (TMHP provider portal / phone) indicated
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-003. Texas Medicaid directs providers to submit prior-authorization requests through the TMHP (Texas Medicaid & Healthcare Partnership) provider portal, by 278 EDI transaction, or by phone using the number on the member ID card. Informational: a cover sheet that names the channel speeds routing. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid prior authorization: requested service is on Texas Medicaid's prior-authorization / notification requirement list
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-004. Texas Medicaid prior authorization applies only to services on its published prior-authorization and notification lists; requesting authorization for a non-listed service is unnecessary. Wave 52-32 ships no bundled list, so the rule is vacuously satisfied with a pointer to the list (it mirrors core R-PA-053 and the the commercial overlay (Aetna through HMSA) and the Medi-Cal / New York Medicaid -004 rules; a later wave bundles the list and flips this to a real membership test). <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid prior authorization: authorization is referenced for a service that requires it before the service date
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-005. Texas Medicaid requires prior authorization to be obtained before a service on its requirement list is rendered; a packet for an authorization-required service should show the authorization / reference number rather than relying on post-service review. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid inpatient prior authorization: admission notification + concurrent / continued-stay clinical review documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-006. Texas Medicaid requires admission notification and conducts concurrent (continued-stay) review for inpatient admissions, collecting the clinical update and discharge plan; an inpatient request should carry that documentation. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid advanced-imaging review: outpatient MRI / CT / PET carries the clinical indication for Texas Medicaid's advanced-imaging utilization-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-007. Texas Medicaid delegates outpatient advanced-imaging (MRI / CT / PET / nuclear cardiology) prior authorization to its advanced-imaging utilization-management program; the request should carry the clinical indication that supports the study. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid expedited / urgent prior authorization: clinical urgency justification present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-008. Texas Medicaid processes an expedited prior-authorization review when a standard timeframe could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function. A request that asks for expedited handling should state that clinical urgency. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid site-of-care review: outpatient surgery / imaging addresses the hospital-outpatient vs. ASC / freestanding site-of-care requirement
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-009. Texas Medicaid's site-of-care medical policy directs certain outpatient surgical and imaging services to an ambulatory surgical center or freestanding facility unless hospital-outpatient care is medically necessary; a hospital-outpatient request should document why that site is required. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-010. Texas Medicaid review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-011. For drugs subject to step therapy Texas Medicaid pharmacy management requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for Texas Medicaid's genetic / molecular lab-management program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-012. Texas Medicaid delegates genetic / molecular testing prior authorization to its lab-management program, which requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid specialty / oncology drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Medical Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-013. Texas Medicaid specialty and oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-014. Texas Medicaid considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-015. Texas Medicaid prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid behavioral health: level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-016. Texas Medicaid behavioral-health prior authorization reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. MCG behavioral-health care guidelines, ASAM criteria); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid transplant prior authorization: routed through a Medicaid-designated transplant center with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-017. Texas Medicaid directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through a Medicaid-designated transplant center / Center of Excellence; a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid experimental / investigational determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-018. Texas Medicaid excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid appeal / reconsideration / state fair hearing: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as an appeal, reconsideration, or state fair hearing, Texas Medicaid asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.tmhp.com/>
findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Texas Medicaid out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.32 R-PA-MCTX-020. findings.evidence: Not a Texas Medicaid packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Medicare FFS hospital-outpatient service on the CMS OPD Required Prior Authorization list: UTN / prior-authorization reference present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.2.1 R-PA-OPD-001. CMS requires prior authorization for certain hospital outpatient department (OPD) services -- blepharoplasty, botulinum toxin injection, panniculectomy, rhinoplasty, vein ablation (2020), cervical fusion with disc removal, implanted spinal neurostimulators (2021), and facet joint interventions (2023) -- before the service is furnished; the provider receives a Unique Tracking Number (UTN) that must appear on the claim. A listed OPD service requested without that authorization / UTN is a likely RFI or denial. <https://www.cms.gov/research-statistics-data-and-systems/monitoring-programs/medicare-ffs-compliance-programs/prior-authorization-and-pre-claim-review-initiatives/prior-authorization-certain-hospital-outpatient-department-opd-services>
findings.evidence: Not a Medicare FFS packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Advanced imaging request: ACR Appropriateness Criteria reference present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-RAD-001. ACR (American College of Radiology) Appropriateness Criteria are the de-facto standard payers use to evaluate imaging-request medical necessity; referencing the matching ACR AC topic in the packet substantially improves first-pass approval rates.
findings.evidence: No radiology CPT (70010-79999) in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Non-emergent MRI: prior conservative management documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-RAD-002. Non-emergent MRIs (especially spine and joint) typically require a documented trial of conservative management -- PT, NSAIDs, activity modification, time -- before payer approval.
findings.evidence: No MRI CPT in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Contrast imaging study: contrast allergy review + renal-function check documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-RAD-003. Iodinated and gadolinium contrast carry hypersensitivity and nephrotoxicity risk; packets requesting a contrast study should document an allergy review and a recent eGFR / SCr.
findings.evidence: No radiology CPT in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Radiology procedure: preceding clinical evaluation referenced
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-RAD-004. Advanced imaging without a documented clinical evaluation (history, physical, working differential) is one of the most common denial reasons; the packet should reference a clinical note dated on or before the imaging request.
findings.evidence: No radiology CPT in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Pediatric imaging: ALARA / radiation-dose consideration documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-RAD-005. For pediatric patients, ALARA (As Low As Reasonably Achievable) principles apply: image Gently / Image Wisely campaigns recommend documenting dose-reduction consideration for CT and nuclear-medicine studies in children.
findings.evidence: No radiology CPT in packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: Surgery with ASA >= 3: anesthesia / pre-op medical clearance documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.5 R-PA-SURG-003. ASA Physical Status >= 3 (severe systemic disease) requires documented pre-operative medical clearance / anesthesia evaluation so the perioperative risk has been assessed by an appropriate clinician.
findings.evidence: No ASA >= 3 anchor; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare prior authorization: NDC documented for a physician-administered (J-code) drug request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-010. UnitedHealthcare / Optum review of physician-administered (buy-and-bill) drugs maps the request to a specific product and dose via the National Drug Code in addition to the HCPCS J-code. Informational: a J-code request without an NDC routes more slowly. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/prior-auth-advance-notification.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare drug prior authorization: step-therapy prior-trial documentation (drugs tried + therapeutic failure) present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-011. For drugs subject to step therapy UnitedHealthcare / OptumRx requires documentation of the prerequisite drugs tried and the therapeutic failure / intolerance for each before the requested agent is approved. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/policies-protocols.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare genetic / molecular testing: the specific test and indication are documented for the molecular-testing notification program
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-012. UnitedHealthcare runs a Genetic and Molecular Testing prior-authorization / notification program that requires the specific test name (and, where applicable, the unique test identifier) plus the clinical indication. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/policies-protocols.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare specialty / injectable drug: the supporting diagnosis is documented for the applicable Drug Policy
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-013. UnitedHealthcare specialty and injectable / oncology drug coverage is governed by drug-specific Medical Benefit Drug Policies that condition coverage on a supporting diagnosis (ICD-10) matching the policy's indication; the request should carry that diagnosis. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/policies-protocols.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare retrospective / retroactive request: a retro-review justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-014. UnitedHealthcare considers a retrospective review only when the service was already rendered for a documented reason (e.g. emergent care, member eligibility not known at the time). A retro request should state that reason. Informational. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/prior-auth-advance-notification.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare DME / home-health prior authorization: a signed, dated written order / plan of care accompanies the request
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-015. UnitedHealthcare prior authorization for durable medical equipment and home-health services requires a written order / plan of care, signed and dated by the ordering provider, before the item / service is dispensed. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/prior-auth-advance-notification.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare behavioral health (Optum / United Behavioral Health): level-of-care criteria documentation present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-016. UnitedHealthcare behavioral-health prior authorization (administered through Optum / United Behavioral Health) reviews the requested level of care against published level-of-care guidelines (e.g. LOCUS / ASAM); the request should document the criteria that support the level requested. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/policies-protocols.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare transplant prior authorization: routed through the UHC transplant / Centers of Excellence network with a transplant-center evaluation
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-017. UnitedHealthcare directs solid-organ and hematopoietic stem-cell transplants through its transplant network / Centers of Excellence (administered with Optum); a transplant prior authorization should reference that routing and carry the transplant-center evaluation. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/prior-auth-advance-notification.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare experimental / investigational / unproven determination: peer-reviewed evidence or a documented policy exception is present
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-018. UnitedHealthcare excludes services its Medical Policy classifies as experimental, investigational, or unproven; a request for such a service must carry the peer-reviewed evidence or the documented exception the policy allows, or it will be denied. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/policies-protocols.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare reconsideration / appeal: references the original determination and adds new clinical information
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-019. When a prior authorization is resubmitted as a reconsideration or appeal, UnitedHealthcare asks the provider to reference the original determination and supply additional clinical information that addresses the denial reason. Informational. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/prior-auth-advance-notification.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

findings.description: UnitedHealthcare out-of-network / network-gap request: a continuity-of-care or network-gap justification is documented
findings.citation: spec-v52 §4.5.8 R-PA-UHC-020. UnitedHealthcare reviews out-of-network prior authorization for a documented network gap or continuity-of-care reason (no participating provider with the required expertise, an active course of treatment). An out-of-network request should state that justification. Informational. <https://www.uhcprovider.com/en/prior-auth-advance-notification.html>
findings.evidence: Not a UnitedHealthcare packet; rule vacuously satisfied.

Medicare sequestration rate (2% of the program-payment portion; Budget Control Act of 2011 Pub. L. 112-25 251A) backing sequestration-adjust. Overridable to model the suspended/phased rates Congress has set.
MPFS surgical payment percentages (assistant at surgery 16%; co-surgeon 62.5% to each; CMS Pub. 100-04 Ch. 12 20.4.3/40.8) backing multi-surgeon-pay.

Multiple-procedure (100% highest / 50% each subsequent) and bilateral (150%) reduction factors and the MPFS MULT PROC / BILAT SURG indicator semantics (CMS Pub. 100-04 Ch. 12 40.6/40.7) backing mppr and bilateral-pay.
